ref: God said, ‚ÄúI command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.‚Äù
text: Na Nyankop…în see d…õ, Nsu no mu mbra mbowa ahorow pii na ntumbowa so nkyin asaase do w…î sor.
translated: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.

ref: ‚ÄúSir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.‚Äù
text: Dabida, mo wura, tsie me: haban no medze ma wo, na …îbo tu a …îw…î mu no medze ma wo; w…î me man mba h…în enyim medze ma wo: sie wo fun.
translated: My daughter, my gold, listen to me: I give you this cloth, and the stone that is in it I give to you; I give you to the children of my nation: bury your shame.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: S…õ m‚Äôegya ne Nyankop…în, Abraham ne Nyankop…în, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m‚Äôekyir a, nky…õ nd…õ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankop…în ehu m‚Äôamandzehu nye me nsaano edwumay…õ, na anadwe yi …îaka w‚Äôenyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahy…õmu
translated: If it were not for my father's God, Abraham's God, and Isaac's Fear, who went with me, surely my father would have sent me away empty-handed today. God has seen my suffering and the work of my hands, and last night he vindicated you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Na …îy…õ m‚Äôakyer…õbaa so ampa, m‚Äôegya ba a, na nny…õ me na ba bi a; na …îb…õy…õ…õ me yer.
translated: Truly, she is my niece, my father's child, and not my mother's child; and she became my wife.

ref: So Isaac told him, ‚ÄúServe me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.‚Äù Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Na …îsee d…õ, Fa pin me nky…õn, ma mindzi me ba ne hanam yi bi, ma me kra nhyira wo. Na …îdze piin ne nky…õn, ma odzii: na …îdze nsa br…õ…õ no, ma …înomee.
translated: Then he said, "Bring the wine close to me, so that I may eat some of my son's meat and my soul may bless you." So he brought it near him, and he ate; and he gave him wine, and he drank.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Na …îsomfo no kaa adze a …îy…õe nyinara kyer…õ…õ Isaak.
translated: And the servant told Isaac all that he had done.

ref: My master answered, ‚ÄúI have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Na …îsee me d…õ, EWURADZE a menam n‚Äôenyim no, …îb…îsoma no b…îfoe ma …îaab…õka wo ho, na …îama wo kwan do ay…õ dwudwoodwo; na eb…õgye yer w…î m‚Äôebusua mu, nye m‚Äôegya fie ama me ba.
translated: And he said to me, The LORD, before whom I walk, will send his angel with thee, and prosper thy way; and thou shalt take a wife for my son of my kindred, and of my father's house.

ref: ‚ÄúIt was the woman you put here with me,‚Äù the man said. ‚ÄúShe gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.‚Äù
text: Na banyin no see d…õ, …îbaa no a edze no b…õkaa mo ho no, …îno na …îmaa me dua no bi, na midzii.
translated: The man said that the woman who was brought to me gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Na h…î mba no bisaa no w…î ne yer ho; na …îsee d…õ, M‚Äôakyer…õbaa a: osiand…õ osuroo d…õ …îb…õse d…õ, Me yer a; na …îsee d…õ, b…õy…õ na osian Rebeka ntsi h…î mba no eku me: osiand…õ no ho y…õ f…õw.
translated: Then the children asked him about his wife; and he said, She is my sister: because he feared to say, She is my wife; lest, said he, the children of the place should kill me for Rebekah's sake: because she was fair to look upon.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, ‚ÄúJacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.‚Äù
text: Na …îtsee Laban ne mbabanyin ns…õm a w…îkae, d…õ, Jakob afa dza …îy…õ h…õn egya dze nyinara; na dza …îy…õ h…õn egya dze na …îdze enya egyapadze yi nyinara.
translated: And he heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, Jacob has taken away all that was our father's; and of that which was our father's has he gotten all this glory.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, ‚ÄúFather, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.‚Äù
text: Na …îno so y…õ…õ edziban a …îy…õ huam, na …îdze br…õ…õ n‚Äôegya; na …îsee n‚Äôegya d…õ, W…îmma m‚Äôegya nso…õr, na ondzi ne ba ne hanam yi bi, ma ne kra nhyira me
translated: He also prepared tasty food and brought it to his father; and he said to his father, "Let my father arise and eat some of his son's game, so that your soul may bless me."

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Na …îmaa efump…înk…î no butubutuuw kurotsia nsubura no ho ewimbibaamu, aber a mbaa pue k…õsaw nsu no.
translated: When the hippopotamus was rolling in the mud near the river bank, the women came out to fetch water.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Na Jakob ne mbabanyin no tsee no, wofii hamu baa fie: na …îy…õ…õ h…în no yaw, na h…în bo fuuw k…õsenara, osiand…õ Sekem ay…õ nkwaseadze w…î Israel d…õ …înye Jakob ne babaa ada; adze a w…înny…õ.
translated: Now when Jacob's sons heard of it, they came from the field: and they were very angry, and they were exceedingly angry, because Shechem had wrought folly in Israel in lying with Jacob's daughter; which thing ought not to be done.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Da no ara mu na EWURADZE nye Abram dzii ahy…õmu see d…õ, W‚Äôasefo na medze asaase yi ama h…în, ofi Egypt esutsen no ho kodu esutsen k…õse no, Eufrates esutsen no ho:
translated: And in the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates:

ref: His brothers asked, ‚ÄúDo you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?‚Äù Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Na no nuanom bisaa no d…õ, Nna tser…õ ampaara d…õ ibedzi h…õn do hen nye no? Ana tser…õ ampaara d…õ ibenya h…õn do tum nye no? Na nkyii w…îtaan no k…õse w…î n‚Äôadaaso no nye ne ns…õm no ntsi.
translated: And his brothers said to him, "Are you truly going to reign over us? Are you really going to have dominion over us?" And they hated him even more because of his dreams and his words.
